brief.objective: explain the PRIME instructional materials correlation process
keyMessages: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
WIDA PRIME information helps educators consider how particular materials fit in their program.
proofPoints: The WIDA PRIME site lists reviewed PRIME v1 and v2 correlations and PRIME 2020 alignments.
The WIDA PRIME site says PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
The WIDA PRIME site says WIDA does not endorse instructional materials and PRIME is not an evaluative tool that judges effectiveness.
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a single-column HTML email announcement with a logo header, concise message, one CTA button, and inline styles.

headlineOptions: Start your WIDA PRIME alignment process
Prepare instructional materials for PRIME review
Help educators understand your materials’ alignment
subheadOptions: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
For publishers, PRIME 2020 focuses on alignment with the WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
The PRIME process helps publishers compile evidence, submit materials for review, and publish approved alignment information.
copy.body: WIDA PRIME provides a defined workflow for publishers preparing instructional materials for review. Publishers contact WIDA before beginning, request and review the workbook, compile evidence, submit instructional materials and a portfolio of evidence, receive WIDA review and a decision debrief, and publish approved alignment information.
bullets: PRIME 2020 offers tools to assist in determining a degree of alignment with the WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
WIDA lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
Materials may be filtered for PRIME V1, PRIME V2, PRIME V2 Español, and PRIME 2020 alignments.
WIDA continues to support WIDA PRIME V2 for Spanish Language Development materials aligned to the WIDA Spanish Language Development Standards, 2013 Edition.
Please contact WIDA before you begin to ensure reviewer availability.

fittedCopy.headline: Start your WIDA PRIME alignment process
fittedCopy.deck: Help publisher teams understand PRIME review, correlations, and instructional-materials alignment.
proofPoints: Clarify PRIME 2020 review for instructional materials.
Show PRIME V1/V2 and Spanish Language correlations.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: email-safe headline with concise body text
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: wide hero art strip with controlled crop
compositionTemplate.copyPlacement: email body stack with 2 proof rows

designRecipe.hierarchy: compact email announcement with a branded visual hook first, then exact headline, deck, two proof rows, and CTA rendered by the app
designRecipe.artDirection: wide WIDA PRIME email hero for publishers, using a polished textless editorial illustration of instructional-material alignment: layered curriculum surfaces, correlation pathways, bilingual language cues, and calm WIDA PRIME color energy sized to read clearly at 680px
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: use subject placement, scale, and negative space to create a clean top-band crop that transitions into the email body; no generated words, logos, UI, buttons, cards, or contact elements
designRecipe.appComposition: app renders official logo header, crops the hero strip, then owns all real headline, deck, proof rows, CTA button, spacing, accessibility, and export-safe email markup
promptDirectives: Design a complete wide email hero concept, not a full-page poster or decorative texture.
Keep all imagery textless and logo-free; no fake newsletter modules, buttons, cards, or contact bars.
Make the alignment/process theme recognizable for publisher audiences at 680px width.
Leave calm breathing room so the app-rendered email body can follow cleanly below.

compositionScore.question: Would I send this to the WCEPS team without apology?

layoutQa.question: Would I send this layout to the WCEPS team without apology?

copyQualityQa.question: Would I send this copy to the WCEPS team without apology?
warnings: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

failureModes.message: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

visualQa.question: Would I send this visual system to the WCEPS team without apology?

renderQa.question: Would I send this rendered artifact to the WCEPS team without apology?

modelQa.question: Would a senior WCEPS reviewer send this artifact without apology?
warnings: Generated hero art is attractive and textless, but primarily shows generic curriculum books and global/social-studies imagery rather than clearly signaling PRIME review, publisher evidence, or correlation workflow.
Proof copy uses the phrase “Spanish Language correlations,” which is less precise and brand-safe than referring to Spanish Language Development materials or standards.
Automated scores are very high despite only moderate brand specificity and some document-spread placeholders that could be perceived as scaffolded content.

Deterministic grid, typography, headline, audience, and CTA.

imageResults.revisedPrompt:
Create a 1200x700 email-safe full-bleed campaign art plate for WIDA PRIME publisher-facing announcement. Wide hero-strip composition, designed to be cropped as a top band above app-rendered email body. Textless and logo-free.

Visual concept: polished editorial illustration / premium education-sector atmosphere showing instructional-material alignment and publisher workflow for K-12 content-area curriculum. A refined tabletop or layered workspace seen from a slightly elevated angle: grade-band curriculum packets, teacher editions and student workbook sample spreads, binders, and review notes arranged in layered depth. Include subtle subject-area cues for science, math, language arts, and social studies through abstract diagrams, color blocks, and image-like shapes only, with absolutely no readable text, letters, numbers, labels, titles, publisher marks, or fake seals. Convey Spanish language development and PRIME 2020/process through careful alignment, correlation, review, and workflow mood, not words.

Composition: top visual band is the strongest hook with layered instructional materials sweeping diagonally from left-center toward right, leaving calm negative space and a clean lower transition area so an email body with headline/deck/proof rows/CTA can follow. Compact email announcement hierarchy; visual-first, minimal density. Create type-safe contrast zones without rendering any typography, UI, cards, modules, CTA, or logo. Wide crop-safe field; avoid tiny details that disappear at email width.

Brand palette: deep blue #0057A8, teal #00A6A6, green #6CC24A, warm gold #F6A800 with soft neutral paper tones. Premium lighting, trustworthy, standards-aligned, publisher-facing, clean, precise. NY media/ad-agency quality, non-generic education storytelling, intentional crop, layered depth, subtle shadows, refined materials.

Strict exclusions: no readable text, no letters, no numbers, no labels, no fake paragraphs, no book titles, no publisher names, no email addresses, no URLs, no phone numbers, no citations, no lorem ipsum. No WIDA PRIME logo, no wordmark, no initials, no logo-like mark, no chevrons, no arrow marks, no mountain marks, no background logo pattern. No official logos/seals/badges/CTAs/headlines. No document layouts, blank boxes, panels, fake cards, mock UI, charts, contact bars, fake badges, fake seals, fake publisher brands, endorsement imagery, close-up faces, detailed hands, stock workshop posing, library stacks, novels, trade books, or template scaffolding. Do not imply endorsement or evaluation of effectiveness.

logoAsset.label: Horizontal full color · transparent · light/image backgrounds

warnings: Copy QA: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

Model QA: Generated hero art is attractive and textless, but primarily shows generic curriculum books and global/social-studies imagery rather than clearly signaling PRIME review, publisher evidence, or correlation workflow.
Model QA: Proof copy uses the phrase “Spanish Language correlations,” which is less precise and brand-safe than referring to Spanish Language Development materials or standards.
Model QA: Automated scores are very high despite only moderate brand specificity and some document-spread placeholders that could be perceived as scaffolded content.

request.goal: explain the PRIME instructional materials correlation process
request.topic: PRIME 2020, PRIME V1/V2, Spanish Language Development correlations, and publisher workflow
request.notes: Do not imply WIDA endorses materials or that PRIME evaluates effectiveness.

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Publishers request and review the workbook, view FAQs, compile evidence and submit for review, receive WIDA review and decision debrief, and publish approved alignment.
sourceEvidence.label: Publisher process

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Please contact us before you begin to ensure reviewer availability.
sourceEvidence.label: Start the process CTA

sourceEvidence.excerpt: PRIME offers tools to assist publishers and educators in determining a degree of alignment with the WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
sourceEvidence.label: PRIME 2020 definition

sourceEvidence.excerpt: To request the WIDA PRIME 2020 Seal, publishers submit instructional materials and a portfolio of evidence for evaluation by WIDA-trained reviewers.
sourceEvidence.label: Publisher submission requirement
